cantonal_cardinality.evidence: SH-LCS-PIPELINE sub-hub defines 1 CANONICAL table (lcs.cid) and 2 SUPPORTING tables (lcs.sid_output, lcs.mid_sequence_state). Error logging routes through parent SUBHUB-CL-LCS error table (lcs.err0) — shared error table per existing LCS architecture. No new error table needed at sub-hub level.

registry_first.evidence: All 3 new tables declared in DB_CHANGESET and column_registry_patch.yml before schema creation. column_registry_patch.yml APPLIED to column_registry.yml (SH-LCS-PIPELINE sub-hub + 5 frame_registry columns). SQL contracts declared in src/sys/lcs/contracts/. Migration 005 applied to Neon.
registry_artifacts: column_registry.yml (patched — SH-LCS-PIPELINE sub-hub added, frame_registry columns added)

id_authority.evidence: communication_id minted by hub-level mintCommunicationId() in src/app/lcs/id-minter.ts. message_run_id minted by hub-level mintMessageRunId() in src/app/lcs/id-minter.ts. No identity minting at spoke or external level. Both ID formats enforced by CHECK constraints in migration 005.

no_sideways_calls.evidence: CID→SID→MID pipeline is entirely within the LCS sub-hub (SUBHUB-CL-LCS). No direct hub-to-hub data flow. Signal ingress comes from spoke signal bridges (lcs.signal_queue). Egress goes through declared adapter layer (src/app/lcs/adapters/). Intelligence read from v_company_intelligence (materialized view within LCS schema). No cross-hub writes.
no_sideways_calls.data_flow: signal_queue → CID compiler → lcs.cid → SID worker → lcs.sid_output → MID engine → lcs.mid_sequence_state + lcs.event → adapter egress

contracts_declared.evidence: All structural contracts explicitly declared as SQL contract files in src/sys/lcs/contracts/. TypeScript type contracts declared in src/data/lcs/types/. All 3 new tables + 1 altered table have both SQL and TypeScript contracts.
